chinese_text: 為學日益，為道日損。損之又損，
translation_text: He who devotes himself to learning (seeks) from day to day to increase (his knowledge); he who devotes himself to the Dao (seeks) from day to day to diminish (his doing).
translation_text_raw: He who devotes himself to learning (seeks) from day to day to increase (his knowledge); he who devotes himself to the Dao (seeks) from day to day to diminish (his doing).

translation_text: He diminishes it and again diminishes it, till he arrives at doing nothing (on purpose).
translation_text_raw: He diminishes it and again diminishes it, till he arrives at doing nothing (on purpose).

translation_text: Having arrived at this point of non-action, there is nothing which he does not do.
translation_text_raw: Having arrived at this point of non-action, there is nothing which he does not do.

translation_text: He who gets as his own all under heaven does so by giving himself no trouble (with that end).
translation_text_raw: He who gets as his own all under heaven does so by giving himself no trouble (with that end).

chinese_text: 及其有事，不足以取天下。
translation_text: If one take trouble (with that end), he is not equal to getting as his own all under heaven.
translation_text_raw: If one take trouble (with that end), he is not equal to getting as his own all under heaven.
